scene.p: A special train, a chair car, occupied by students. An upperclassman is attempting to sleep.

tr.td: Things have quieted down and I’ll get a chance for a nap. He dozes.
Hey, wake up! All out for Hunkusville!
Aw, set on a tack. He dozes.

td.stage-direction: A Freshman begins blowing a horn.

tr.td: Enough is too darned much! He chases all the Freshmen out. He dozes.

td.stage-direction: Somebody drops the brasses of the brass drum.

tr.td: Who⁠—what⁠—hey, what time is it?
One-thirty, suh.
Fine. Everybody’s asleep now. Now for a good nap. He dozes.

td.stage-direction: The train whistles for a station.

tr.td: Curses! He dozes.

scene.p: A few minutes later. A flock of girls come through.

tr.td: Supposedly singing. I gotta gal, her name is Lulu! I love Lulu, I love Lulu, darling!
Jumps seven feet out of seat. Ye gods, what next!
Seventeenth verse, same as the fist, I love Lulu⁠ ⁠… Exit.
Applesauce. He dozes.
Still singing. Seven hundredth verse, same as the fist, I love Lulu, I love Lulu, darling!

td.stage-direction: Upperclassman develops deep and enduring hatred for the name Lulu.

tr.td: Hey, what time is it?
Two-thirty, suh.
How much longer before we pull in?
One two hours, suh.
Here’s a nice place to sit; you don’t mind do you? They sing. Eight hundredth verse, same as the first⁠—
No, I don’t mind. Grinds teeth and bites hunks out of chair arm.
Seven thousandth verse, same as the first, I gotta girl, her name is Lulu, I love Lulu⁠—
Conductor, is there no chance at all for a train robbery, holdups, murders and all that you know?
No chance at all, sir.
Darn.
